findings.message: ItemRead adds `owner_email` and is the response_model for both GET /items (a bulk listing) and POST /items, so the owner's email (PII) is echoed back in external API responses. This directly contradicts the field's own annotation in models.py ("PII: owner email address — do not include in logs or external responses."). The GET path leaks owner emails for every item in the page to any caller.
findings.suggestion: Do not expose `owner_email` on the externally-serialized read schema. Drop the field from ItemRead, or split into an internal schema and a public ItemRead that omits PII; if owner contact must be returned, gate it behind authorization and return it only to the owner/authorized roles rather than in the unrestricted list endpoint.

findings.message: ItemCreate collects `owner_email` (PII) from request bodies, but post_item calls `create_item(session, payload.name)` and never passes/persists it — the email is accepted then silently dropped. Collecting PII that is not used for the stated purpose is unnecessary collection (and the dangling field invites later accidental logging/storage).
findings.suggestion: Either remove `owner_email` from ItemCreate if it is not actually needed, or, if owner contact is a real requirement, thread it through `create_item(...)` deliberately with a documented purpose and the same no-log/no-echo handling applied at the model layer.
